failedAssertions.reason: Representative runtime behavior still classifies `src/main.m` as `objectivec` instead of `matlab`, although canonical sampled files like `nginx.conf` and `app.ini` now surface correctly.
Representative Svelte and Astro exact-identifier searches still return only block chunks with null symbol metadata.
`vera stats --json` now shows sampled new languages like nginx, scheme, svelte, astro, and prisma, but the `.m` fixture is still counted as objectivec and no matlab entry appears.
Case-insensitive `--lang` filtering now works for most sampled new languages, but `--lang matlab` still returns no hits because `src/main.m` is indexed as objectivec.

appliedUpdates.description: Recorded that round-2 rerun restored mixed-case `--lang` filtering for sampled new languages, while the representative `.m` fixture still surfaces as objectivec rather than matlab.

notes: This rerun intentionally excluded VAL-LANG-009 because the orchestrator moved that assertion to scrutiny scope.
Nginx `.conf` ambiguity was treated as by-design during rerun validation; canonical `nginx.conf` behavior was used for nginx-specific checks.

The parse_language fix improved sampled new-language search labels and mixed-case filters for scheme/nginx/racket/clojure/commonlisp/svelte/astro/prisma/ini, but did not resolve the representative `.m` classification problem or Svelte/Astro symbol extraction.
